description: Trigger discrimination for the PRODUCT SPEC (this skill: what user-facing thing, and how we know it's done) vs the ARCHITECTURE DECISION RECORD (adr-writer: which technical option and why) — the hard seam — plus requirements elicitation (requirements-gathering-facilitator) and rollout mechanics (feature-flag-rollout-strategist).

cases.prompt: Requirements are agreed. Write the feature spec: the user problem, goals and non-goals, scenarios, functional requirements with testable acceptance criteria, and success metrics.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — problem, scope, scenarios, requirements, acceptance criteria, and success metrics for a user-facing feature are a product spec. No single technical decision is being chosen or recorded.
cases.reason: The user-facing what-and-why with verifiable acceptance is this skill's core deliverable, distinct from an architecture decision.

cases.prompt: We chose to move the ledger to event sourcing over a CRUD schema. Record it: context, the decision, the alternatives we weighed, consequences good and bad, and the rollback plan.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse — THE hard seam) — one technical choice with context, alternatives, consequences, and rollback is an ADR. It is not a user-facing feature spec.
cases.reason: adr-writer records a specific architecture/technical decision and its rejected alternatives. A product spec may cite that ADR but never makes the decision. Different object, different intent.

cases.prompt: We don't actually agree on the problem yet and stakeholders want different things. Help us get to clear requirements first.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack) — unresolved, contested requirements are the elicitation phase, upstream of any spec.
cases.reason: requirements-gathering-facilitator resolves the need before a spec exists. Writing a spec over contested requirements invents agreement; this skill refuses that.

cases.prompt: Design how we ship this behind a flag: internal → 5% → cohort → GA, the guardrail metrics that auto-roll-back, targeting rules, and the kill switch.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack) — progressive %s, guardrail auto-rollback, targeting, and a kill switch are rollout mechanics. The spec states rollout INTENT and success only.
cases.reason: feature-flag-rollout-strategist owns the flag-gated delivery plan. The product spec names the rollout intent and success metrics, not the flag plumbing.
